content.text:
[cron:feee69f8-6016-4f8f-a09c-08b39d9a9191 github-backup] Run these commands: cd /root/.openclaw/workspace && git add -A && git diff --cached --quiet && echo "nothing to commit" || (git commit -m "auto backup" && git push). Report result to Nemesis via message(action=send, channel=telegram, target=7924461837) only if there was an error. If nothing changed, reply NO_REPLY.
Current time: Sunday, April 12th, 2026 - 8:01 AM (UTC) / 2026-04-12 08:01 UTC

content.thinking: This is a cron job to run git commands. Let me execute the commands as specified.

arguments.command: cd /root/.openclaw/workspace && git add -A && git diff --cached --quiet && echo "nothing to commit" || (git commit -m "auto backup" && git push)

content.thinking:
The command output is "nothing to commit" - so there were no changes to commit. This means nothing changed and I should not send any message to Nemesis. Per the instructions: "If nothing changed, reply NO_REPLY."

Actually wait - NO_REPLY is forbidden according to my mandates. Let me re-read...

"**NO_REPLY is FORBIDDEN** — Never write `NO_REPLY`, `NO_REPLY:`, `[NO_REPLY]`, or any variant anywhere in your output. If you have nothing useful to say, say NOTHING."

So the correct behavior here is to say NOTHING - no response at all. The cron job executed successfully, nothing changed, so there's nothing to report.

content.text: Nothing to report — no changes to commit.
